title: Fix Mermaid rendering for nested empty subgraphs
body_text:
## Summary
- fix Mermaid subgraph traversal to include nested prefixes even when they have no internal edges
- preserve deterministic prefix ordering while expanding missing ancestor prefixes
- add regression coverage for `parent:child:grandchild` nested-empty-subgraph rendering

## Testing
- Not run in this environment: local Python is 3.9, while this repo requires 3.10+ for test imports.

Closes #35492

entities.name: Mermaid
entities.description: A diagramming tool whose rendering functionality is being fixed.

entities.name: Internal Edges
entities.description: Connections within a subgraph in Mermaid, relevant to the condition for including nested prefixes.

entities.name: Deterministic Prefix Ordering
entities.description: A desired property for the consistent ordering of prefixes in Mermaid, which the fix aims to preserve.

entities.name: Missing Ancestor Prefixes
entities.description: A condition where parent prefixes are not correctly expanded, affecting deterministic prefix ordering.

entities.name: Python 3.9

entities.description: A type of testing added to ensure that previously fixed bugs related to nested-empty-subgraph rendering do not re-emerge.

entities.name: Testing

extracted_data.summary: This issue fixes Mermaid rendering for nested empty subgraphs by improving subgraph traversal to include nested prefixes and preserving deterministic prefix ordering, with regression coverage added, though testing was skipped due to Python version incompatibility.
